expectations: Includes the correct winget install command: winget install JanDeDobbeleer.OhMyPosh --source winget
Includes the PowerShell profile init line: oh-my-posh init pwsh | Invoke-Expression
Explains how to open or edit the PowerShell profile (e.g. using $PROFILE or notepad $PROFILE)
Mentions reloading the profile or restarting the terminal after changes
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step guide: install via winget, edit PowerShell profile to add init line, install a Nerd Font, reload profile.
evals.prompt: I want to set up oh-my-posh on my Windows machine. I use PowerShell.

expectations: Includes the Homebrew install command using the tap: brew install jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/oh-my-posh
Includes the zsh init line: eval "$(oh-my-posh init zsh)"
Mentions adding the init line to ~/.zshrc
Warns about macOS Terminal color/icon limitations or recommends a modern terminal like iTerm2
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step guide: install via Homebrew with the tap command, add init line to ~/.zshrc, install a Nerd Font, warn about macOS Terminal limitations.
evals.prompt: I'm on macOS and use zsh. How do I get oh-my-posh working?

expectations: Explains using the --config flag to specify a theme
Provides a concrete init command example with a theme name (e.g. oh-my-posh init pwsh --config 'jandedobbeleer')
Mentions how to browse available themes (link to ohmyposh.dev/docs/themes)
Mentions exporting a theme for local editing (oh-my-posh config export)
evals.expected_output: Explains --config flag, shows how to set a theme in the init line, links to theme browser or oh-my-posh debug, mentions exporting a theme for editing.
evals.prompt: I just installed oh-my-posh. How do I change the theme to something that shows git status?
